summary: Large item-decomp progress PR touching 25 files across common item code and many individual item TUs. The concrete build-status win was moving `melee/it/items/itbombhei.c` from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py`; the decomp.dev report recorded +0.38% matched code, +14840 matched-code bytes, 32 new matches, and 34 improvements in still-unmatched items. The PR also refined symbol metadata, local/static data scope, struct typing, function signatures, inline helpers, stack-shaping, and expression ordering to close small but numerous item matching gaps. The PR body only says remaining item TUs/functions counts, so motivation beyond continued item progress is inferred from the diff and bot report.

key_files.role: Adjusted item data/rodata/sdata/sdata2 symbol sizes, scopes, local labels, and split data objects.
Added `AnimBundle` and changed boomerang attribute fields to bundle animation/matanim/shapeanim pointers.
Improved item-spawner and item-state matches via temporary variables, pointer aliases, boolean ordering, and local variable scoping.
Matched multiple common item functions through static data, function-pointer casts, signature fixes, stack/color temporaries, volatile argument handling, and control-flow reshaping.
Updated `fn_80277D8C` prototype to include the third unused argument.
Added collision dynamics structs and made local-variable/layout changes in item collision code.
Primary newly matching item TU, including data and several Bombhei motion/collision functions.
Improved Dosei functions/data with inline rotation/facing helpers and matching-oriented refactors.
Adjusted Link arrow animation helpers and rodata/data matching.
Improved boomerang functions using normalized-angle and helper extraction patterns.
Large set of hookshot matching improvements across many hunks.
Matched or improved Samus missile physics/shield-bounce and turn-clamp code.
Improved Seak chain functions with named clamp/load inline helpers.

what_changed: Updated 25 files with 936 additions and 800 deletions, mostly under `src/melee/it` and `src/melee/it/items`.

Adjusted many `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` item symbols: some globals became local `@NNN` labels, rodata sizes changed from 0x10 to 0xC, `it_803F6A78` was split into `lbl_803F6A78` plus `it_803F6A84`, and several sdata2 constants became local floats/doubles.
Added `AnimBundle` in `itCharItems.h` and changed two `itLinkBoomerangAttributes` fields from single `HSD_AnimJoint*` pointers to bundled animation data.
Added `ItCollDynamicsDesc` and `ItCollDynamics` structs in `itcoll.c`, giving typed names to collision-dynamics data that had likely been anonymous/opaque before.
Changed `fn_80277D8C` from a two-argument function to `void fn_80277D8C(HSD_MObj*, u32, u32)` and cast it to `HSD_MObjSetupFunc` when assigning `it_803F1F90.setup`.
Made `it_803F5440` static in `it_2725.c`, matching the symbol-map shift from global to local/static scope.

Reshaped matching-sensitive code in common item functions: pointer-to-field aliases in `it_8026CB9C`, explicit boolean accumulation in `it_8026D3CC`, stack/local placement in `it_8026E32C`, union command temporaries in `it_802790C0`, and goto-based selection flow in `it_8027AB64`.
Changed `it_8027B288` to take `volatile u32 arg1`, removed an intermediate volatile union, and added `PAD_STACK(4)` to preserve stack/codegen shape.

Introduced or renamed many static inline helpers in item TUs, including Bombhei setup/animation helpers, Dosei rotation helpers, Link bomb/boomerang helpers, Samus missile clamp/rotation helpers, Seak chain clamp helpers, and Seak needle ground-hit/collision helpers.

The decomp.dev bot report credited the PR with 32 new matches, including Bombhei `.data`, several Bombhei functions, `it_8026D3CC`, Dosei data/functions, Mewtwo Shadow Ball functions/data, Samus missile functions/sdata2, Link bomb rodata/animation, and common item functions such as `fn_80277D8C`, `it_80278108`, `it_802790C0`, and `it_8027B288`.
smart_moves: Promoted only the TU with full evidence, `itbombhei.c`, to Matching rather than overclaiming broader item progress.

Kept symbols in sync with codegen realities: string/constant data that no longer needed global names was marked local, while the mixed data at `803F6A78` was split into a string label and float data object.

Used small, matching-directed C rewrites instead of large semantic changes: local variable reorderings, aliases, explicit temporaries, delayed declarations, and boolean accumulation all target instruction scheduling/register allocation.
For callback compatibility, changed `fn_80277D8C` to the apparent real callback shape with an unused third argument, while casting the assignment to the library setup-function type.
Used a stack-relative color pointer in `it_80278108` (`GXColor* color = (GXColor*) ((u8*) &sp1C - 4)`) to reproduce a compiler stack layout without pretending it is a clean high-level variable.
Rewrote recent-Pokemon selection in `it_8027AB64` with cached recent values, pointer assignment to `Item_804A0E24.y`, and a `goto selected` label to better match original control flow.
Added named structs such as `AnimBundle`, `ItCollDynamicsDesc`, and `ItCollDynamics` where data layout evidence was strong enough, improving future readability without requiring full semantic naming.
decomp_lessons: Item matching often advances through many tiny codegen fixes across many TUs; a PR can be valuable even when most changed files remain NonMatching.
Do not leave static strings/floats/doubles as global `it_80...` symbols when the compiler emits them as local literals; use `@NNN` local labels and update `symbols.txt` accordingly.
When a data symbol contains heterogeneous content, split it into appropriately typed labels/objects rather than forcing one oversized object definition.
Callback signatures should match the actual call ABI, including unused trailing parameters; assignment casts can be necessary when project typedefs lag exact callback shape.

For MWCC matching, variable declaration order and scope matter. Moving `chk2` inside the conditional, introducing `union CmdUnion* u`, or splitting counters into `cnt`/`cnt2` can affect register allocation.
Volatile parameters and `PAD_STACK` remain legitimate tactics for reproducing stack layout when evidence points to compiler artifacts rather than source-level logic.

Inline helper extraction is a reusable tactic, but helper names should encode the matched behavior or target function when semantics are still weak, e.g. `it_802A013C_inline`, `itSeakChain_clamp_x10`, or `itSamusMissile_ClampTurn`.
Header struct improvements can be matching-critical: representing repeated anim/matanim/shapeanim triples as `AnimBundle` changed Link boomerang attribute layout assumptions.
naming_conventions: Individual item files keep the lowercase item prefix pattern: `itbombhei`, `itdosei`, `itlinkarrow`, `itlinkbomb`, `itlinkboomerang`, `itlinkhookshot`, `itsamusmissile`, `itseakchain`, `itseakneedlethrown`.
Unidentified functions retain address-style names such as `it_8026CB9C`, `it_8027AB64`, `fn_80277D8C`, and `fn_8028007C` until stronger semantic names are known.

Inline helper names often combine the target function/motion name with an `inline` or behavior suffix, e.g. `fn_80280974_inline`, `itBombhei_UnkMotion1_Setup`, `itDosei_UnkMotion4_Rotate`, `itSamusMissile_ClampTurn`.
Local compiler-emitted data symbols use `@NNN` in `symbols.txt`, while address labels such as `lbl_803F6A78` are used when a data address is a label/string rather than a globally named object.
New layout structs used descriptive but conservative names: `AnimBundle`, `ItCollDynamicsDesc`, and `ItCollDynamics`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed object build status from NonMatching to Matching only after the TU was fully matched (`itbombhei.c`).
Adjusted symbol sizes/scopes in `symbols.txt` to match emitted data sections exactly, including local string/float/double constants.
Introduced pointer aliases to structure fields before allocation in `it_8026CB9C` to influence store/load codegen: `u8** item_kinds` and `u16** weights`.
Reordered boolean OR evaluation in `it_8026D3CC` by seeding from Heart, then OR-ing Tomato and Foods, rather than using one chained return expression.
Declared locals closer to use sites to change live ranges and stack/register allocation.
Used explicit function-pointer casts for callback assignments.
Removed unnecessary casts in comparisons where typed pointers already carried the needed type, e.g. `tobj_toon != NULL` and `tobj_toon->imagedesc != NULL`.
Used `volatile u32` and `PAD_STACK(4)` to recover exact codegen in `it_8027B288`.
Used labels/goto in `it_8027AB64` to match original loop-exit control flow.
Created or renamed static inline helpers in item TUs to force repeated instruction sequences and callsite/inlining shapes.

review_feedback.body_summary: Reported +0.38% matched code, +14840 matched-code bytes, +0.39% linked code, 32 new matches, and 34 improvements in unmatched items for GALE01.

review_feedback.note: No inline review comments were present in the slice, so there is no human rationale beyond the approval and automated metrics.

follow_up_queries: Which remaining item TUs after PR #2533 were still NonMatching?
Find later PRs that also modify `fn_80277D8C` or `HSD_MObjSetupFunc` callback signatures.
Find PRs that reuse `AnimBundle` or further refine `itLinkBoomerangAttributes`.
Find later progress on `itdosei.c`, `itlinkhookshot.c`, `itseakchain.c`, and `itsamusgrapple.c` after this partial improvement.
Search for additional symbol-map local-scope conversions around `it_803F6A78`, `it_804DC840`, and related item constants.
Compare later item PRs for repeated volatile-argument and `PAD_STACK` matching tactics.
